t: Bespoke <button> in TaskTimeWarningPopup instead of canonical Button
Raw hex literal VACATION_COLOR in AllUsersCalendar bypasses design tokens
QuickWorkTimer chip buttons bypass canonical Button (radio-group pattern)
calculateCurrentTotalMinutes: timeAdjustments not sanitized, can add to already-summed manualMinutes+timerMinutes
startTask / resumeTask write timerStartedAt with new Date() before any await, but Firestore commit is async — clock skew between client and server is unguarded
calculateCurrentTotalMinutes: live timer delta uses new Date() instead of getLithuanianNow(), inconsistent with all other elapsed-time paths

endSession quickWork/call partial-segment double-count: the restored session's FULL duration is written to work_sessions AND the task's manualMinutes, but partial segment is a separate work_session with isQuickWork:true
archiveOldTasks cutoff comparison: task with no completedAt, confirmedAt, or updatedAt is silently skipped
computeWorkerStats planned-shift window check uses getLithuanianDateString(p.start) where p.start may be a raw ISO string rather than a Date
pausedSession nesting only goes one level deep in endSession restore path
handleLegacyLogging for call and quickWork has no per-write catch on the critical setDoc/addDoc pairs
Rule C — TaskTimeLimitPopup uses full-saturation red outside the quick-work state
Rule A — BreakTimer compact button loses visible text label when SideRail is collapsed (hideLabel=true)
Hardcoded Firebase API key and config committed to git history
Disabled user can still update their own user doc (non-security fields)
Worker can self-confirm a task on CREATE via call/quick-work path (design choice with no server-side owner check)
Legacy inline request_notifications writes bypass notify() funnel and the commentText size clamp
Orphan rule: shift_logs — locked but never accessed by the client
